HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

Searching...

Triangle.cpp
Go to the documentation of this file.

6#include "Triangle.h"
7#include <iostream>
8
9// ---------------- Constructors ----------------
10// Construct with nodes and domain indices; compute geometry cache.
11Triangle::Triangle(rvec *n1, rvec *n2, rvec *n3, int dIndex1, int dIndex2) {
12 nodes.push_back(n1);
13 nodes.push_back(n2);
14 nodes.push_back(n3);
15
16 domainIndeces.push_back(dIndex1);
17 domainIndeces.push_back(dIndex2);
18
19 CalculateGeometryData();
20}
21
22// Construct with nodes; domains can be set later.
23Triangle::Triangle(rvec *n1, rvec *n2, rvec *n3) {
24 nodes.push_back(n1);
25 nodes.push_back(n2);
26 nodes.push_back(n3);
27
28 CalculateGeometryData();
29}
30
31// Set front/back domain indices; recompute geometry cache.
32int Triangle::SetDomainIndeces(int dIndex1, int dIndex2) {
33 domainIndeces.push_back(dIndex1);
34 domainIndeces.push_back(dIndex2);
35 CalculateGeometryData();
36 return 0;
37}
38
39// Swap first two nodes (invert orientation).
40int Triangle::Invert() {
41 rvec *nTmp;
42 nTmp = nodes.at(0);
43 nodes.at(0) = nodes.at(1);
44 nodes.at(1) = nTmp;
45 return 0;
46}
47
48// ---------------- Geometry cache ----------------
62int Triangle::CalculateGeometryData() {
63 normalVector = cross((rvec)(*(nodes[1]) - *(nodes[0])),
64 (rvec)(*(nodes[2]) - *(nodes[0])));
65 surfaceArea = sqrt(dot(normalVector, normalVector));
66 normalVector /= surfaceArea;
67 surfaceArea /= 2.;
68 center = 0;
69 perimeter = 0;
70 double l[3];
71 for (int i(0); i <= 2; ++i) {
72 center += Node(i) / 3.;
73 l[i] = sqrt(dot(Node((i + 1) % 3) - Node((i + 2) % 3),
74 Node((i + 1) % 3) - Node((i + 2) % 3)));
75 perimeter += l[i];
76 }
77 I1 = 0;
78 double &A = surfaceArea, p = perimeter / 2;
79 for (int i(0); i <= 2; ++i) {
80 double a = l[i], b = l[(i + 1) % 3], c = l[(i + 2) % 3];
81 I1 += log(1 - a / p) / a;
82 I2s[i] =
83 A * A / 30 *
84 ((10 + 3 * (c * c - a * a) / (b * b) + 3 * (b * b - a * a) / (c * c)) *
85 a -
86 (5 - 3 * (a * a - b * b) / (c * c) - 2 * (b * b - c * c) / (a * a)) *
87 b -
88 (5 - 3 * (a * a - c * c) / (b * b) - 2 * (c * c - b * b) / (a * a)) *
89 c +
90 (a * a - 3 * b * b - 3 * c * c - 8 * A * A / (a * a)) * 2 *
91 log(1 - a / p) / a +
92 (a * a - 2 * b * b - 4 * c * c + 6 * A * A / (b * b)) * 4 *
93 log(1 - b / p) / b +
94 (a * a - 2 * c * c - 4 * b * b + 6 * A * A / (c * c)) * 4 *
95 log(1 - c / p) / c);
96 I2d[i] =
97 A * A / 60 *
98 ((-10 + (c * c - a * a) / (b * b) + (b * b - a * a) / (c * c)) * a +
99 (5 + (a * a - b * b) / (c * c) - 6 * (b * b - c * c) / (a * a)) * b +
100 (5 + (a * a - c * c) / (b * b) - 6 * (c * c - b * b) / (a * a)) * c +
101 (2 * a * a - b * b - c * c + 4 * A * A / (a * a)) * 12 *
102 log(1 - a / p) / a +
103 (9 * a * a - 3 * b * b - c * c + 4 * A * A / (b * b)) * 2 *
104 log(1 - b / p) / b +
105 (9 * a * a - 3 * c * c - b * b + 4 * A * A / (c * c)) * 2 *
106 log(1 - c / p) / c);
107 }
108 I1 *= -4. / 3 * A * A;
109 return 0;
110}
111
112// ---------------- Cached getters & simple ops ----------------
113double Triangle::getI1() { return I1; }
114
115double Triangle::getI2(rvec *r) {
116 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
117 if (nodes[i] == r) return I2s[i];
118 }
119 exit(1);
120 return 0;
121}
122
123double Triangle::getI2(rvec *r1, rvec *r2) {
124 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
125 if (nodes[i] != r1 and nodes[i] != r2) return I2d[i];
126 }
127 exit(1);
128 return 0;
129}
130
137double Triangle::getI2Translated(rvec r1, rvec r2, rvec t) {
138 rvec r1t(r1 - t);
139 const double eps(1e-20);
140 if (dot(r1t - r2, r1t - r2) < eps) {
141 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
142 if (dot(*nodes[i] - r2, *nodes[i] - r2) < eps) return I2s[i];
143 }
144 } else {
145 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
146 if (dot(*nodes[i] - r1t, *nodes[i] - r1t) > eps and
147 dot(*nodes[i] - r2, *nodes[i] - r2) > eps)
148 return I2d[i];
149 }
150 }
151#ifdef DEBUG
152 std::cout << r1 << r2 << t << "\n";
153 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
154 std::cout << *nodes[i] << "\n";
155 }
156#endif
157 exit(1);
158 return 0;
159}
160
161// ---------------- Basic accessors ----------------
162// Node value by index.
163rvec Triangle::Node(int index) { return *(nodes[index]); }
164
165// Node pointer by index.
166rvec *Triangle::NodePtr(int index) { return nodes[index]; }
167
168// Return FRONT/BACK if dIndex matches, otherwise −1.
169int Triangle::DomainPosition(int dIndex) {
170 if (domainIndeces[FRONT] == dIndex)
171 return FRONT;
172 else if (domainIndeces[BACK] == dIndex)
173 return BACK;
174 else
175 return -1;
176}
177
178// Reference unit normal (points to FRONT).
179rvec Triangle::RefNormalVector() { return normalVector; }
180
181// FRONT/BACK normal.
182rvec Triangle::FBNormalVector(int side) {
183 if (side == FRONT)
184 return normalVector;
185 else
186 return -normalVector;
187}
188
189// Area (cached).
190double Triangle::Area() { return surfaceArea; }
191
192// Perimeter (cached).
193double Triangle::Perimeter() { return perimeter; }
194
195// Centroid (cached).
196rvec Triangle::Center() { return center; }
197
198// Pair {front, back} domain indices.
199std::pair<int, int> Triangle::BorderingIndeces() {
200 return std::pair<int, int>(domainIndeces.at(0), domainIndeces.at(1));
201}
202
203// Replace node pointers and recompute geometry cache.
204int Triangle::AssignNodes(rvec *n1, rvec *n2, rvec *n3) {
205 nodes.clear();
206 nodes.push_back(n1);
207 nodes.push_back(n2);
208 nodes.push_back(n3);
209 CalculateGeometryData();
210 return 0;
211}
212
213// Analytic R^2 integral over triangle (self term), used in some kernels.
214double Triangle::IntRR() {
215 rvec xp, yp, zp, rc;
216 double jacobian;
217 double mA[3][3], mB[3][3];
218 double result;
219
220 xp = *(nodes.at(1)) - *(nodes.at(0));
221 yp = *(nodes.at(2)) - *(nodes.at(0));
222 zp = cross(xp, yp);
223 jacobian = sqrt(dot(zp, zp));
224 for (int i(0); i < 3; i++) {
225 mA[i][0] = xp(i);
226 mA[i][1] = yp(i);
227 mA[i][2] = zp(i);
228 }
229 for (int i(0); i < 3; i++) {
230 for (int j(0); j < 3; j++) {
231 mB[i][j] = 0.;
232 {
233 for (int k(0); k < 3; k++) mB[i][j] += mA[k][i] * mA[k][j];
234 }
235 }
236 }
237 rc = rvec((mA[0][0] + mA[0][1]) / 3., (mA[1][0] + mA[1][1]) / 3.,
238 (mA[2][0] + mA[2][1]) / 3.);
239 result = jacobian *
240 ((mB[0][0] + mB[1][1]) / 12. + (mB[0][1] + mB[1][0]) / 24. +
241 dot(*(nodes.at(0)), rc) + dot(*(nodes.at(0)), *(nodes.at(0))) / 2.);
242 return result;
243}
244
245// Replace domain index n1 with n2 if present.
246int Triangle::SwitchIndex(int n1, int n2) {
247 if (domainIndeces[0] == n1) domainIndeces[0] = n2;
248 if (domainIndeces[1] == n1) domainIndeces[1] = n2;
249 return 0;
250}
251
252// ---------------- Adjacency / common entities ----------------
253// Count shared nodes with triangle T (0..3).
254int Triangle::FindAdjacency(Triangle *T) {
255 int ret = 0;
256 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
257 for (int j = 0; j < 3; j++) {
258 if (NodePtr(i) == T->NodePtr(j)) {
259 ret++;
260 break;
261 }
262 }
263 }
264 return ret;
265}
266
267// Find common edge (local indices) with T; return {i+1, -(j+1)} or {0,0}.
268std::pair<int,int> Triangle::FindCommonEdgeIndices(Triangle* T) {
269 for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
270 int iEdgeNode1 = i, iEdgeNode2 = (i + 1) % 3;
271 for (int j = 0; j < 3; ++j) {
272 int jEdgeNode1 = j, jEdgeNode2 = (j + 1) % 3;
273 if ( ( this->NodePtr(iEdgeNode1) == T->NodePtr(jEdgeNode1) &&
274 this->NodePtr(iEdgeNode2) == T->NodePtr(jEdgeNode2) ) ||
275 ( this->NodePtr(iEdgeNode1) == T->NodePtr(jEdgeNode2) &&
276 this->NodePtr(iEdgeNode2) == T->NodePtr(jEdgeNode1) ) ) {
277 return { i + 1, -(j + 1) };
278 }
279 }
280 }
281 return { 0, 0 }; // no common edge
282}
283
284// Find common vertex (local indices) with T; return {i+1, j+1} or {0,0}.
285std::pair<int,int> Triangle::FindCommonVertexIndices(Triangle* T) {
286 for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
287 for (int j = 0; j < 3; ++j) {
288 if (this->NodePtr(i) == T->NodePtr(j)) {
289 return { i + 1, j + 1 };
290 }
291 }
292 }
293 return { 0, 0 }; // no common edge
294}
295
296// Count shared nodes with T after translating T by vector t.
297int Triangle::FindAdjacency(Triangle *T, rvec t) {
298 int ret = 0;
299 double eps = 1e-4;
300 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
301 for (int j = 0; j < 3; j++) {
302 rvec rij(Node(i) - t - T->Node(j));
303 if (dot(rij, rij) < eps) {
304 ret++;
305 break;
306 }
307 }
308 }
309 return ret;
310}
311
312// ---------------- Point-in-triangle ----------------
322bool Triangle::containsPoint(const rvec &p, double tol)
323{
324 double A = Area();
325
326 // Degenerate triangle
327 if (std::fabs(A) < tol) return false;
328
329 // Coplanarity: distance from p to the triangle's plane
330 double dist = dot(p - Node(0), normalVector);
331 if (std::fabs(dist) > tol) return false;
332
333 // unit vectors
334 rvec unitX = Node(1) - Node(0); unitX /= sqrt( dot(unitX, unitX) );
335 rvec temp = Node(2) - Node(0);
336 rvec unitZ = cross(unitX, temp); unitZ /= sqrt( dot(unitZ, unitZ) );
337 rvec unitY = cross(unitZ, unitX);
338
339 // vertices in triangle plane
340 std::vector<rvec> nodesInPlane;
341 nodesInPlane.push_back( rvec( 0.0, 0.0, 0.0 ) ); // Node(0) in plane
342 temp = Node(1) - Node(0);
343 nodesInPlane.push_back( rvec( clean( dot(temp, unitX) ),
344 clean( dot(temp, unitY) ), 0.0 ) ); // Node(1) in plane
345 temp = Node(2) - Node(0);
346 nodesInPlane.push_back( rvec( clean( dot(temp, unitX) ),
347 clean( dot(temp, unitY) ), 0.0 ) ); // Node(2) in plane
348
349 // Observation point in triangle plane
350 rvec pos( clean( dot( unitX, p - Node(0) ) ),
351 clean( dot( unitY, p - Node(0)) ), 0.0 );
352
353 // Simplex coordinates
354 std::vector<double> zeta(3);
355 for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
356 zeta[i] =
357 ( ( nodesInPlane[(i + 1) % 3][0] * nodesInPlane[(i + 2) % 3][1] -
358 nodesInPlane[(i + 2) % 3][0] * nodesInPlane[(i + 1) % 3][1] ) +
359 pos[0] * ( nodesInPlane[(i + 1) % 3][1] - nodesInPlane[(i + 2) % 3][1] ) +
360 pos[1] * ( nodesInPlane[(i + 2) % 3][0] - nodesInPlane[(i + 1) % 3][0] ) ) /
361 ( 2. * A );
362 }
363
364 // Check if point is in triangle (allow a tiny epsilon outside)
365 if (zeta[0] < -tol || zeta[1] < -tol ||
366 zeta[2] < -tol || (zeta[0] + zeta[1] + zeta[2]) > 1.0 + tol)
367 return false;
368
369 return true;
370}
